name: Pumpkin Eaters Doughnuts from Fred Goslin
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, cream the shortening and sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
3. Mix in the pumpkin and cereal thoroughly.
4. Let stand for 2 minutes.
5. Sift together flour, baking powder, spice, and salt.
6. Stir into the pumpkin mixture, half at a time.
7. Cover and chill for 1 hour or until dough is stiff enough to handle.
8. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough to 1/2 inch thickness.
9. Cut with 2-1/2 inch doughnut cutters.
10. Deep fry in a large pan with 2 inches of oil at 375°F (190°C).
11. Fry for about 1 minute per side or until thoroughly cooked and golden brown.
12. Drain on paper towels over a wire rack.
13. Repeat with the trimmings to use all of the dough.
14. Dip cooled doughnuts in cinnamon sugar or powdered sugar if desired.
